person_name: Honoratus, Count of the East under Constantius II (fl. mid-4th century)
person_name_full: Honoratus, Count of the East under Constantius II (fl. mid-4th century)
notes: Count of the East under Emperor Constantius II in the mid-4th century, noted for his pertinacious constancy and administrative role.

docs.title: BOOK XIV
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: 3. This circumstance was also a proof, and that no doubtful or concealed one, of the cruelty of his nature, that he took delight in cruel sports, and in the circus he would rejoice as if he had made some great gain, to see six or

docs.title: Letter 5003
docs.author: Cassiodorus
docs.subject_summary: Book 5, Letter III: HONORATO V. I. QUAESTORI
docs.excerpt: … for the quaestor must not only be learned in the law but must also be able to express the king's will in language worthy of the dignity of the royal chancery. A clumsy letter reflects ill on the sender; a well-crafted one brings honor to the throne.

docs.title: Letter 2027
docs.author: Ennodius of Pavia
docs.excerpt: … writes as if separated by oceans — a playful complaint about negligent correspondence from a neighbor. To Honoratus, from Ennodius. You live in the neighborhood, and yet you write as though continents divided us — which is to say, you do not write at all.
